title: hyoseris radiata
intro: Hyoseris radiata, also known as the moon carrot or smallflower false foxglove, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Hyoseris radiata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried flowers and leaves often used in herbal remedies.
